---
title: "Wand Goldenrod (Solidago virgata)"
slug: "solidago-virgata"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354559060/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Wand Goldenrod"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354559060/medium.jpg" alt="Wand Goldenrod" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/animalgrljen">
animalgrljen</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/200910713">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=855020)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in a variety of habitats including flatwoods, open woodlands, and disturbed areas.

## Natural history notes

A perennial wildflower characterized by its upright, wand-like stems and clusters of small yellow flower heads arranged along the upper portion of the plant. In Florida, it is found in a variety of habitats including flatwoods, open woodlands, and disturbed areas, often in sandy soils. It typically blooms in late summer to fall and provides an important nectar source for pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/855020-Solidago-virgata)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3967)
